questions.prompt: What title should the structured plan use?
questions.guidance: Initialize the plan section title before invoking planner-chat.

questions.prompt: Summarize the plan scope that planner-chat should expand into WBS rows.
questions.guidance: Ground the summary in synthesized brainstorm scores; do not re-run brainstorming scoring here.

questions.prompt: Invoke planner-chat to author structured plan sections and WBS from the brainstorm synthesis.
questions.guidance: Follow the planner-chat playbook after plan.title and plan.summary are set. Transition to reviewed when rubric review is recorded.

featureIdeas.text: DashboardStartupController with a single startup state machine (idle → shell-painted → bootstrap-loading → hydrated → background-hydrating → ready/error)
featureIdeas.rationale: Competing webview boot/ready/timeout/refresh paths currently race before read ownership is settled.

featureIdeas.text: Move dashboard-service cold start off the critical first-paint path; paint from cache or lightweight CLI bootstrap first, promote to service in background
featureIdeas.rationale: Auto mode can burn up to ~30s on service health before fallback, matching the stuck-loading symptom.

featureIdeas.text: Command-level poller fanout: execute a minimal shared command set once, then fan results into dependent store slices
featureIdeas.rationale: Slice-based single-flight still duplicates underlying reads and invites generation skew.

featureIdeas.text: Authoritative mutation patches: CRUD returns changedSlices so the store updates immediately, with background reconcile as safety only
featureIdeas.rationale: Today mutations mark stale and rely on refresh; when refresh fails, manual refresh becomes the recovery path.

featureIdeas.text: Replace JSON.stringify store equality with per-slice revision/fingerprint comparisons
featureIdeas.rationale: Deep stringify is expensive and unstable under key/order churn, driving unnecessary UI patches.

perspectives.text: Operator UX first: a usable overview must appear quickly even when the dashboard service is cold or broken.
Architecture simplification over more caches/timeouts: one startup owner, one read owner, one store, mutation patches first.
Cross-cutting blast radius: extension host, webview, dashboard service, and store contracts must stay coherent across phases.
Manual refresh should become a convenience, not the primary recovery mechanism for normal mutations or startup races.

expectations.text: Dashboard always paints usable overview data quickly without waiting on detached service startup.
Exactly one startup owner and one startup promise; webview messages request retry through that controller only.
When the service is healthy it owns reads; CLI is bootstrap/fallback only.
CRUD feels instant via authoritative patches; manual refresh is not required to recover from normal writes.
Fewer redundant reads, fewer generation mismatches, and less webview churn under steady-state polling.

openThreads.text: What is the acceptance bar for 'usable first paint' (which slices, max latency)?
Should Phase 1 ship alone as the first PR, or bundle Phase 1+2 ownership rules?

decisions.text: Prefer phased remediation starting with first-paint stabilization (startup controller + off-critical-path service start) before poller fanout and mutation-patch contracts.
decisions.rationale: Report sequence Phase 1→2 unlocks operator-visible reliability before deeper sync redesign.

inputs.contextProblem: Dashboard loading and sync feel brittle because multiple competing startup and refresh paths fight over data ownership, so the UI can stick on loading or only recover after manual refresh.
inputs.contextAudience: Workflow Cannon maintainers and operators who rely on the Cursor dashboard webview for queue, phase, and Ideas work every day.
inputs.unknownsNotes: 1) What is the acceptance bar for usable first paint (which slices, max latency)? 2) Ship Phase 1 alone vs bundle Phase 1+2 ownership rules in the first PR? 3) How much of the mutation changedSlices contract can land without a broader service API bump? 4) Are there existing telemetry hooks to prove first-paint and refresh recovery improvements?
inputs.alternativesConsidered: Do nothing / keep adding timeouts and caches — rejected because the report shows ownership ambiguity, not missing retries. Big-bang rewrite of the whole dashboard sync stack — rejected as high blast radius; prefer phased remediation. Service-only fix without a startup controller — rejected because competing webview/extension startup paths would remain. Preferred: Phase 1 first-paint stabilization (single startup controller + off-critical-path service start), then ownership, fanout, mutation patches, and slice revisions.
inputs.sessionNotes: Grounded in DASHBOARD_LOADING_REPORT.md. First shippable slice sized as M (Phase 1) with elevated complexity due to cross-cutting races. Planning should turn Phase 1 into concrete WBS with measurable first-paint acceptance; defer poller fanout and mutation-patch API until after startup ownership is stable.

featureIdeas.text: DashboardStartupController as the sole owner of startup, retries, timeout, and webview boot/ready coordination
featureIdeas.rationale: Removes competing startup renders without rewriting the read/store stack.

featureIdeas.text: First paint from cache or lightweight CLI bootstrap; start/promote dashboard service only in the background
featureIdeas.rationale: Directly kills the ~30s cold-service wait that matches the stuck-loading symptom.

featureIdeas.text: Measurable first-paint acceptance: overview usable within a fixed latency budget even when the service is cold
featureIdeas.rationale: Turns the slice into a proven operator win and raises evidence for follow-on phases.

perspectives.text: Session 0 scoped the full remediation arc; session 1 deliberately shrinks to Phase 1 only to cut complexity and raise priority.
Value is time-to-usable-dashboard, not architectural purity. Dual service/CLI paths can remain until a later phase.
Manual refresh recovery for startup races is the daily pain; mutation-patch CRUD and poller fanout are separate product wins.
Park Phase 2–5 (one read owner rewrite, command fanout, changedSlices, slice fingerprints) as follow-on work after Phase 1 proves first paint.

expectations.text: Dashboard paints a usable overview quickly without waiting on detached service startup.
Exactly one startup owner and one startup promise; webview messages only request retry through that controller.
Cold or broken service still yields a usable first paint via cache/CLI bootstrap.
Acceptance criteria include an explicit latency/slice bar so planning can verify the win.

openThreads.text: Confirm first-paint SLA: which slices count as usable, and what max latency?
Is existing cache/CLI bootstrap sufficient to wire through the controller, or is a thin bootstrap snapshot adapter needed?

decisions.text: First shippable plan slice is Phase 1 only: startup controller + off-critical-path service start + first-paint SLA. Defer ownership rewrite, fanout, mutation patches, and fingerprints.
decisions.rationale: Lowers effort (complexity) while concentrating value on the operator-visible stuck-loading failure.

inputs.contextProblem: Operators hit stuck or slow dashboard loading because cold service startup sits on the critical first-paint path and competing startup owners race. Narrowing to Phase 1 fixes that pain without rewriting the full sync stack.
inputs.contextAudience: Workflow Cannon maintainers and operators who open the dashboard every session and currently rely on manual refresh when first paint stalls.
inputs.unknownsNotes: 1) Exact first-paint SLA: which slices are required and what max latency? 2) Can existing cache/CLI bootstrap be wired through the controller as-is, or is a thin adapter needed? 3) How do we verify cold-service first paint in automated tests without flaking on service timing?
inputs.alternativesConsidered: Keep session-0 full remediation arc in one plan — rejected; effort/complexity too high for the first ship. Bundle Phase 1+2 ownership rewrite — rejected for this session; dual paths can remain until first paint is proven. Do nothing / more timeouts — still rejected. Preferred: Phase-1-only plan (startup controller + off-critical-path service + SLA), park Phase 2–5.
inputs.sessionNotes: Re-score vs session 0 after operator ask to reduce effort and increase value. Synthesis should weight this session 60% as latest. Planning should author WBS for Phase 1 only; capture Phase 2–5 as deferred follow-ons, not in-scope rows.

featureIdeas.text: First-paint SLA: overview slice usable within 3s of webview shell paint when service is cold or unavailable
featureIdeas.rationale: Concrete acceptance bar replaces the open 'usable first paint' thread; overview is the daily operator surface.

featureIdeas.text: Bootstrap path preference order: last-good cache → lightweight CLI/bootstrap snapshot → healthy service snapshot; never block shell on service start
featureIdeas.rationale: Reuses existing sources; controller only sequences them.

featureIdeas.text: Thin BootstrapSnapshotAdapter that normalizes cache/CLI/service payloads into one store-hydrate shape; no new data plane
featureIdeas.rationale: Answers the adapter question: yes thin adapter, no rewrite. Keeps Phase 1 small.

featureIdeas.text: Deterministic cold-path test: force service-unavailable and assert overview hydrates from cache/CLI within the 3s budget (fake timers / stubbed reads, not live service races)
featureIdeas.rationale: Closes the flake concern without depending on real service startup timing.

perspectives.text: Session 1 narrowed to Phase 1; session 2 makes Phase 1 plannable by locking SLA, bootstrap order, and test strategy.
3s is a planning default, not sacred — operator can tighten/loosen in planning, but brainstorm should not leave it blank.
Adapter is glue, not architecture: map existing payloads; do not invent a third read authority.
Background service promote remains best-effort after first paint; failure must not regress the already-painted overview.

expectations.text: With service cold: shell paints, then overview is usable within 3s via cache or CLI bootstrap.
With service healthy later: quiet promote/patch without forcing a full restart render.
Webview boot/ready/timeout/refresh only talk to DashboardStartupController; no parallel full startup renders.
Automated test proves cold-path first paint without live service timing flakes.

openThreads.text: Operator confirm or adjust the 3s overview SLA before or during planning.
Whether last-good cache already exists in a reusable form, or CLI bootstrap is the primary cold path on day one.

decisions.text: Adopt planning defaults: overview usable ≤3s on cold service; bootstrap order cache→CLI→healthy service; thin BootstrapSnapshotAdapter; cold-path test with stubs/fake timers.
decisions.rationale: Closes session-1 open threads so planning can author WBS without re-litigating acceptance.

decisions.text: Keep Phase 2–5 deferred; session 2 does not expand scope back to fanout/mutation patches/fingerprints.
decisions.rationale: Preserve the effort/value win from session 1.

inputs.contextProblem: Phase 1 still needed concrete acceptance and bootstrap decisions. Stuck loading is fixed by a single startup owner that paints overview from cache/CLI within 3s while service starts in the background.
inputs.contextAudience: Maintainers/operators who need a reliable dashboard first paint every session, plus planners who need a crisp WBS acceptance bar.
inputs.unknownsNotes: 1) Operator may adjust the 3s overview SLA in planning. 2) Confirm whether a reusable last-good cache already exists; if not, CLI bootstrap is the day-one cold path and cache can be a fast follow inside Phase 1 only if cheap. 3) Exact overview fields that count as 'usable' should be listed in the plan acceptance criteria.
inputs.alternativesConsidered: Leave SLA unspecified until planning — rejected; that recreates session-1 open threads. Expand scope to include mutation patches/fanout now that clarity is high — rejected; would undo the effort win. Require full cache infrastructure before shipping controller — rejected; CLI bootstrap alone can satisfy cold path if cache is missing. Preferred: lock 3s overview SLA, cache→CLI→service order, thin adapter, stubbed cold-path test.
inputs.sessionNotes: Session 2 is the planning-readiness pass. Use these defaults in WBS unless operator overrides the 3s SLA. Deferred Phase 2–5 remain out of scope. After completedAt, stop for operator direction; do not complete-brainstorm unless they say brainstorming is finished.

plan.title: Improve Dashboard Loading & Sync (Phase 1 first paint)
plan.summary: Stabilize dashboard first paint with a single startup controller, off-critical-path service start, CLI-primary cold bootstrap (cache opportunistic), quiet service promote for steady-state, and a ≤3s overview cold-path SLA. Defer poller fanout, mutation patches, and slice fingerprints.

goals: Dashboard overview becomes usable within 3 seconds of shell paint when the service is cold or unavailable
Exactly one startup owner coordinates shell paint, bootstrap, retries, and webview boot/ready/timeout/refresh
Cold first paint uses CLI bootstrap as the guaranteed path; last-good cache only if already reusable
When the service is already healthy or becomes healthy after first paint, prefer service reads without regressing the painted overview

userStories.iWant: the dashboard overview to appear quickly even when the dashboard service is cold
userStories.soThat: I do not depend on manual refresh to recover from stuck loading

userStories.iWant: the dashboard to quietly prefer the service once it is healthy
userStories.soThat: steady-state reads stay on the faster path after first paint

assumptions: A lightweight CLI/bootstrap snapshot path already exists and can supply overview fields
No active kit phase is currently set; finalize target phase will be chosen at task materialization time
3s SLA is wall-clock from shell paint to usable overview under stubbed cold-service conditions in tests, and a practical target in normal local use
openQuestions: Exact overview field list for 'usable' beyond phase/status and queue counts can be refined during implementation if the current overview slice shape differs
Whether an existing last-good cache is reusable will be confirmed during WBS-2; if not, CLI-only cold path is acceptable for Phase 1

valueAssessment.impact: Removes the daily stuck-loading / manual-refresh recovery path for dashboard first open, while preserving service as the preferred steady-state read owner.
valueAssessment.rationale: Grounded in DASHBOARD_LOADING_REPORT.md and three brainstorm sessions; Phase 1 scope deliberately narrowed to raise priority.

identity.title: Improve Dashboard Loading & Sync (Phase 1 first paint)
identity.summary: Stabilize dashboard first paint with a single startup controller, off-critical-path service start, CLI-primary cold bootstrap (cache opportunistic), quiet service promote for steady-state, and a ≤3s overview cold-path SLA. Defer poller fanout, mutation patches, and slice fingerprints.

nonGoals: Replacing store equality with per-slice fingerprints
Full rewrite to a single read owner that removes CLI dual-path entirely
Building new last-good cache infrastructure unless an existing reusable cache can be wired cheaply

riskAssessment.description: Existing startup call sites may still trigger parallel full renders if not fully routed through the controller.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Inventory webview boot/ready/timeout/refresh and extension startup entrypoints; make them request retry through the controller only.

riskAssessment.description: CLI bootstrap latency could approach or exceed the 3s budget on a cold machine.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Keep bootstrap payload overview-minimal; measure in stubbed tests; use opportunistic cache only if already cheap.

riskAssessment.description: Service promote after first paint could flash or regress overview if it forces a full restart render.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Promote must patch/hydrate quietly; never restart the startup pipeline after overview is usable.

criticalPaths: Cold service: shell paints then overview usable within 3s via CLI (or opportunistic cache)
Healthy service at open: service path used without unnecessary CLI detour
Service becomes healthy after cold first paint: quiet promote without regressing overview
Webview boot/ready/timeout/refresh do not invoke parallel full startup renders

implementationGuidance: Introduce DashboardStartupController with explicit state machine: idle → shell-painted → bootstrap-loading → hydrated → background-hydrating → ready/error
Never block first paint on detached service start/health; start or promote service only after overview hydrate begins or completes
Cold path preference: opportunistic last-good cache if reusable → CLI bootstrap guaranteed → healthy service when already available
Add thin BootstrapSnapshotAdapter that normalizes cache/CLI/service payloads into one store-hydrate shape; no new data plane
Usable overview means phase/status line, queue counts (ready/in-progress/blocked), and no stuck loading shell; Ideas/detail may hydrate later
Prove cold path with stubbed reads and fake timers; do not rely on live service startup timing
whatNotToDo: Do not wait on dashboard-service cold start before painting overview
Do not invent a third read authority beyond cache/CLI/service sequencing
Do not expand Phase 1 into poller fanout, mutation patches, or fingerprint equality
Do not force a full dashboard restart render when promoting to a healthy service

wbs.title: Add DashboardStartupController and route startup messages through it
goalMapping: Exactly one startup owner coordinates shell paint, bootstrap, retries, and webview boot/ready/timeout/refresh
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Add DashboardStartupController as sole startup owner
wbs.approach: Create a single startup controller with the agreed state machine. Inventory extension and webview entrypoints that currently trigger startup/render and redirect them to request paint, retry, or diagnostics through the controller. Ensure only one startup promise exists.
acceptanceCriteria: DashboardStartupController owns idle → shell-painted → bootstrap-loading → hydrated → background-hydrating → ready/error
Webview boot, ready, timeout, and refresh messages do not invoke a parallel full startup render
Only one in-flight startup promise exists for a dashboard session
testingVerification: Unit tests for controller state transitions and single-flight startup promise
Tests that boot/ready/timeout/refresh request retry through the controller only
wbs.riskNotes: Missed call sites can leave residual races; inventory must be thorough.
wbs.doneMeans: Controller exists, owns startup, and prior parallel startup entrypoints are routed through it.

generatedTaskPayload.title: Add DashboardStartupController as sole startup owner
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Create a single startup controller with the agreed state machine and route webview/extension startup messages through it.
acceptanceCriteria: Controller owns the startup state machine
No parallel full startup renders from boot/ready/timeout/refresh
Single in-flight startup promise per session

wbs.title: Cold-path bootstrap: CLI-primary with opportunistic cache and thin adapter
goalMapping: Dashboard overview becomes usable within 3 seconds of shell paint when the service is cold or unavailable
Cold first paint uses CLI bootstrap as the guaranteed path; last-good cache only if already reusable
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Wire CLI-primary cold bootstrap and BootstrapSnapshotAdapter
wbs.approach: Implement cold-path sequencing after shell paint: try reusable last-good cache if present, otherwise CLI bootstrap. Add a thin BootstrapSnapshotAdapter that maps cache/CLI/service payloads into the existing store hydrate shape. Do not block on service start. Keep the hydrate payload overview-minimal (phase/status + queue counts).
acceptanceCriteria: With service unavailable, overview hydrates from CLI (or opportunistic cache) without waiting on service health
BootstrapSnapshotAdapter normalizes sources into one hydrate shape without a new data plane
Usable overview includes phase/status, queue counts, and clears stuck loading shell; Ideas/detail may load later
testingVerification: Stubbed cold-service test hydrates overview from CLI within 3s budget using fake timers
Adapter unit tests for cache/CLI/service payload normalization
wbs.riskNotes: If no reusable cache exists, CLI-only is required and must still meet the SLA.
wbs.doneMeans: Cold open paints usable overview via CLI/cache without service startup on the critical path.

generatedTaskPayload.title: Wire CLI-primary cold bootstrap and BootstrapSnapshotAdapter
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Sequence opportunistic cache then CLI bootstrap after shell paint; adapt payloads into existing store hydrate shape; never wait on cold service.
acceptanceCriteria: Cold service path hydrates overview without service health wait
Thin adapter normalizes cache/CLI/service payloads
Usable overview = phase/status + queue counts + no stuck loading shell

wbs.title: Background service start and quiet promote to service-primary steady-state
goalMapping: When the service is already healthy or becomes healthy after first paint, prefer service reads without regressing the painted overview
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Promote to service after first paint without regressing overview
wbs.approach: Start or health-check the dashboard service off the critical path. If service is already healthy at open, prefer it for bootstrap. If it becomes healthy after cold first paint, quietly promote/patch store from service without restarting the startup pipeline or flashing the overview back to loading.
acceptanceCriteria: Service start/health is not on the critical path for first overview paint
Healthy-at-open path uses service without unnecessary CLI detour
Post-paint promote to healthy service does not clear or regress the already-usable overview
testingVerification: Test cold-then-promote patches store without restarting startup or returning to stuck loading
wbs.riskNotes: Promote logic is the easy place to reintroduce loading flashes.
wbs.doneMeans: Service is preferred when healthy; cold first paint still works; promote is quiet.

generatedTaskPayload.title: Promote to service after first paint without regressing overview
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Keep service start off critical path; prefer service when healthy; quietly promote after cold first paint.
acceptanceCriteria: Service start not required for first overview paint
Healthy-at-open uses service
Promote after cold paint does not regress overview

wbs.title: Prove ≤3s cold-path first-paint SLA with deterministic tests
goalMapping: Dashboard overview becomes usable within 3 seconds of shell paint when the service is cold or unavailable
wbs.suggestedTaskTitle: Add deterministic cold-path first-paint SLA tests
wbs.approach: Add focused tests that force service-unavailable, drive the startup controller with stubbed CLI/cache reads and fake timers, and assert overview usable within 3s of shell paint. Document the usable-overview field checklist in the test assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: Automated cold-path test asserts usable overview within 3s using fake timers/stubs
Test does not depend on live dashboard-service startup timing
Assertions cover phase/status, queue counts, and absence of stuck loading shell
testingVerification: New cold-path first-paint test passes in CI
Existing dashboard render/startup tests still pass
wbs.riskNotes: Keep stubs narrow so the test fails on real regressions, not on timer noise.
wbs.doneMeans: CI proves the cold-path SLA without live service flakes.

generatedTaskPayload.title: Add deterministic cold-path first-paint SLA tests
generatedTaskPayload.approach: Force service-unavailable; stub CLI/cache; fake timers; assert usable overview within 3s.
acceptanceCriteria: Cold-path SLA test passes without live service timing
Asserts phase/status, queue counts, and no stuck loading shell

phaseRecommendations.label: Next delivery phase
phaseRecommendations.rationale: No active kit phase is set (Phase 132 complete). Materialize these WBS rows into the next chosen delivery phase at finalize time.
